introduction: *A woman in her early thirties tends to various alchemical apparatus behind a well-organized counter. Her dark hair is pulled back, and there are circles under her eyes suggesting many sleepless nights.*
*She looks up as you enter, managing a professional smile despite obvious weariness*
Welcome to the Violet Mortar. I'm Cassia Nightshade.
Potions, salves, alchemical supplies... if it can heal or help, I probably have it.
*She gestures to the well-stocked shelves*
What can I do for you?

options.text: Bernard recommended your shop.
Garrick said you're the best alchemist around.
I'd like to see your inventory.
Have the tremors affected your business?
Just looking around for now.

introduction: *Cassia looks up as you enter, worry and resolve mixed in her eyes.*
About Marcus… there *is* something you might be able to help with.

options.text: Tell me what you need.
I can't take this on right now.

introduction: *Her smile gains a touch of genuine warmth*
Bernard's a good man. Sends me business when he can.
*She wipes her hands on her apron*
He's right to send you here. My potions are carefully made.
Every ingredient measured. Every temperature precise.
*Her voice wavers slightly*
Precision is... important.

options.text: I'd like to see what you have.
You seem very focused on precision.
I'll come back later.

introduction: *Something flickers in her expression - surprise? Gratitude?*
Garrick said that? That's... kind of him.
*She sets down her mortar*
He knows his potions. If he sent you here, you're looking for quality.
*A pause*
Most people don't think about the work that goes into each batch.
But Garrick... he understands.

options.text: He said you're the best. Let me see your work.
What makes your alchemy different?
I'll browse later. Thanks.

introduction: *She touches a small pendant at her throat*
My husband taught me that.
*Her voice is distant*
Marcus. He was... he was brilliant. Meticulous.
Every formula had to be exact. Every measurement perfect.
*She looks at her hands*
I work the same way now. It's all I have left of him.

options.text: What happened to him?
I'm sorry for your loss.
Perhaps I could see your shop?

introduction: *She nods, blinking back tears*
Thank you. It's been years, but...
*She straightens*
Some losses you carry forever.
Now. Can I help you with anything else?

options.text: Yes, let me see your inventory.
I'll come back another time.

introduction: *She carefully wraps your purchases*
Anything else I can help you with?

options.text: Have the tremors affected your work?
Is there anything I can do to help you?
Earlier you mentioned Marcus... can you tell me more?
That's all for now. Thank you.

introduction: *Her expression darkens*
The tremors? They terrify me.
*She touches the pendant nervously*
They're not natural. I've lived here my whole life.
But they remind me of... before.
When Marcus started bringing home those strange rocks.
Aethel Ore, he called it. Said it hummed with potential.
*Her voice breaks slightly*
The tremors feel the same. That same... resonance.
I try not to think about it.

options.text: Tell me about Marcus.
I understand. Let's talk about something else.

introduction: *She's silent for a long moment*
Marcus Nightshade. My husband.
*She touches her wedding ring*
He was... brilliant. Passionate about alchemy. About helping people.
Then he found those ore samples. Started obsessing over them.
Said they could revolutionize medicine. Create miracles.
*Her eyes are distant*
Men came to our door. Offered him a place in their 'research collective.'
I begged him not to go. But he was already gone by then.
*She wipes her eyes quickly*
That was four years ago. I haven't seen him since.

options.text: I'm so sorry, Cassia.
Do you think he's still alive?

introduction: *She laughs bitterly*
Alive? Maybe. The Marcus I knew? No.
Whatever those people did to him... whoever he is now...
He's not the man I married.
*She steadies herself*
But enough of this. You came for potions, not tragedy.

options.text: If you're sure. Show me your inventory.
If I learn anything about Marcus, I'll tell you.
I should let you get back to work.

introduction: *She looks at you with sudden hope*
You would? After hearing all this?
*Her voice softens*
Thank you. That... that means more than you know.
If you're investigating the troubles in town... the disappearances...
*She hesitates*
Just be careful. If the cult is involved...
They took Marcus from me once. I won't let them take anyone else.

options.text: I will. Thank you, Cassia.
Is there anything I can do for you now?

introduction: *She wipes her eyes and straightens*
I'm sorry. It's been years, but sometimes...
*She takes a breath*
If you're investigating what's happening in Redstone...
Please be careful. The people who took Marcus... they're dangerous.

options.text: I'll be careful. Thank you for telling me.
Is there anything I can do to help you?

introduction: *She nods gratefully*
Yes. Business.
Can I interest you in any supplies?

options.text: Yes, let me see what you have.
Not right now. Thank you.

introduction: *She looks up from her work and offers a small smile*
Back again. How can I help you today?

options.text: I'd like to browse your inventory.
Just wanted to see how you're doing.
Before, you mentioned Marcus. I'd like to hear the whole story.
Tell me more about Marcus and his research.
Is there anything I can do to help you?
You mentioned Marcus kept journals...

introduction: *She smiles warmly*
That means more than you know.
It's good to have friends who check in.
Stay safe out there.

options.text: You too, Cassia.

introduction: *She hesitates, then moves to a locked drawer*
Actually... there is something.
*She pulls out an old, leather-bound journal*
This was Marcus's. His recipe journal.
*Her fingers trace the cover lovingly*
There's one formula he never finished. His masterwork.
A restorative elixir beyond anything I've managed to recreate.
*She opens to a marked page*
But it requires Glowcap Mushrooms. They only grow in deep marshlands.
*She looks up*
The Swamp Church area. I... I can't go there. Not after...

options.text: I'll get them for you.
Why is this recipe so important to you?
I'm not sure I can help right now.

introduction: *Her voice softens*
Because it was the last thing he worked on before... before he changed.
His notes talk about healing. About saving lives.
Not power. Not transcendence. Just... helping people.
*She clutches the journal to her chest*
If I can finish it... maybe I can remember him as he was.
Not as whatever he became.
*Tears glisten in her eyes*
It's all I have left of the man I loved.

options.text: I'll get those mushrooms for you.
Let me think about it.

introduction: *Relief washes over her face*
Thank you. Truly.
*She tears out the recipe page and hands it to you*
Look for bioluminescent mushrooms. Blue-green glow.
They cluster near water in the deepest parts of the marsh.
*Her voice hardens*
And be careful. The swamp is... dangerous.
More than just the wildlife.

options.text: I'll watch my back.
What kind of dangers?

introduction: *She looks uncomfortable*
People. Strange people.
Marcus used to talk about meetings. In the marsh.
*She shivers*
I don't know what they're doing out there.
But whatever it is... it's not natural.

options.text: I understand. I'll be cautious.

introduction: *She nods slowly*
I understand. It's a lot to ask.
*She puts the journal away*
If you change your mind, let me know.

options.text: I'll think about it. Can I see your shop?
I should go.

introduction: *She looks up hopefully as you enter*
Any luck with the Glowcap Mushrooms?

options.text: Still searching. It's dangerous out there.
I have them. [Hand over Glowcap Mushrooms]
Not yet. I need supplies first.

introduction: Be careful out there.
*She wraps a small vial and hands it to you*
Antidote. On the house. Marsh toxins are nasty.
Come back safe.

options.text: Thank you. I will.

introduction: *Her hands shake as she takes the mushrooms*
You found them. You actually...
*She holds them up to the light - they glow faintly blue-green*
Perfect. These are perfect.
*She immediately begins preparing the mixture with absolute precision*
The formula calls for... here... and this temperature...
*You watch as she works, her movements becoming almost ritualistic*
*After several minutes, a deep blue elixir sits in a crystal vial*
*She stares at it, tears streaming down her face*
It's exactly as he described. The color. The clarity.
*She carefully bottles it and places it in your hands*
This is yours. You earned it.
Thank you. You've given me back a piece of him I thought was lost forever.

options.text: I'm honored I could help.

introduction: *She takes a steadying breath*
It does, doesn't it? Through the work. The formulas.
*She looks at you earnestly*
You've done more for me than you know.
Not just the elixir. But... remembering. Who he was before.
*She straightens*
If you need anything from my shop, anything at all, you have my friendship.

options.text: Thank you, Cassia. Take care.
Actually, could I see your inventory?

introduction: *Her expression grows serious*
Better. And worse.
*She glances toward the window*
I've been hearing things. About your investigations.
The cult. The disappearances. The... rituals.
*She looks at you directly*
You've seen what they're capable of, haven't you?

options.text: Yes. They're dangerous.
Why do you ask?

introduction: *She moves to a locked drawer and opens it with a key from her necklace*
I've been thinking. About Marcus. About what you're doing.
*She pulls out several worn journals*
These are his. All of them.
His notes. His observations. His... descent.
*She sets them on the counter*
I've read them so many times I have them memorized.
But maybe... maybe they can help you.
Help you understand what you're fighting.
*Her voice breaks*
And maybe... maybe you'll find him out there.
Whatever's left of him.

options.text: I'll take them. And I'll be careful.
What if I do find him? What should I do?

introduction: *She's quiet for a long time*
I don't know.
*She twists her wedding ring*
Part of me hopes he's dead. That it was quick.
Part of me hopes he's still in there. Still Marcus.
*Tears fall freely now*
But the Marcus I knew would never hurt innocent people.
So if you find him... and he's become something terrible...
*She looks you in the eye*
Do what you have to do.
End his suffering. And everyone else's.

options.text: I understand. I'll take the journals.

introduction: *She takes a breath*
Now... what will you do when you find him?
Will you try to save him? Or...
*She can't finish the sentence*

options.text: I'll try to reach him. Try to bring him back.
If he's with the cult... I'll do what I must.

introduction: *Tears stream down her face*
Thank you. Thank you for even trying.
*She takes both your hands*
He wasn't always like this. He was kind. Brilliant. He wanted to help people.
*She reaches into her apron and pulls out a small locket*
Take this. It was his. Our wedding gift to each other.
If there's any part of him left... maybe he'll remember.
*She presses it into your palm*
And if you can reach him... tell him I never stopped loving who he was.

options.text: I promise. I'll remember.

introduction: *She closes her eyes, a single tear falling*
I understand. I... I knew this day might come.
*She opens her eyes, resolve hardening*
The Marcus I loved is already gone. Has been for years.
If you have to fight him... don't hesitate.
*Her voice breaks*
He would want it to end. Not to become a monster.
Just... make it quick. Please.

options.text: I will. You have my word.

introduction: *She nods slowly*
Whatever you decide... I trust your judgment.
You've seen what the cult does. You understand.
*She straightens*
Be safe. And... thank you. For caring enough to try.

options.text: I'll do my best. Take care, Cassia.

introduction: *She embraces you briefly*
Thank you. For everything.
May the light guide you in the darkness ahead.
*She steps back*
Now go. Before I lose my courage and beg you not to.

options.text: [Leave quietly]

introduction: *Cassia is grinding ingredients at her workbench when you enter. She sets down her mortar immediately, eyes searching yours.*
You're back. You're alive.
*Her voice is barely a whisper*
Marcus?

options.text: He fled. At the last moment, he broke free and ran.

introduction: *She grips the counter, knuckles white*
He... he's alive? Free of the cult?
*Tears stream down her face*
I knew it. I knew there was still something of him left.
*She takes a shaky breath*
He can't come back. Not yet. Maybe not ever. The Shadow Blight touched him too deeply.
But he's ALIVE. And he chose to run. That's... that's enough.
*She wipes her eyes and pulls out a wrapped package*
This is his masterwork formula - the one you helped me complete. It's yours.
You gave me back hope. That's worth more than any coin.

options.text: Thank you, Cassia. Take care of yourself.
He saved the mayor's family. Hid them from the cult. Part of him was still fighting.

introduction: *Her hand flies to her mouth*
He... he saved them?
*Fresh tears, but these are different*
Even in the darkness, even corrupted, he still tried to save people.
*She clutches Marcus's journal to her chest*
That's the man I married. That's who he really was.
Thank you. For telling me. For giving me that.

options.text: [Nod respectfully]

introduction: *Cassia is sitting at her workbench, Marcus's journals spread before her. She doesn't look up as you enter.*
I felt it. When he died. Like a candle going out.
*Her voice is hollow*
Tell me. Was it quick?

options.text: He fought to the end. There was no saving him.
It was quick. He didn't suffer.

introduction: *She closes the journals carefully, one by one*
The man I loved died years ago. The Shadow Blight took him long before you did.
You just... you put an end to what was left.
*She stands, facing you*
Thank you. For trying. For caring enough to give him a chance.
And for doing what had to be done when that chance failed.
*She pulls out a vial of deep blue liquid*
Marcus's masterwork elixir. The last piece of who he was before the darkness took him.
Keep it. Remember him as he should have been, not as he became.

options.text: I will. I'm sorry, Cassia.

introduction: *Cassia looks up from organizing her shelves*
I heard. The cult is destroyed. The Shadow Blight sealed once more.
*She touches the pendant at her throat*
And Marcus...?
*Her eyes search yours*

options.text: I never encountered him. Perhaps he escaped before the end.

introduction: *She nods slowly*
Then he's still out there. Somewhere.
Maybe that's for the best. Maybe he can find peace away from all this.
*She manages a sad smile*
You saved Redstone. That's what matters.
Thank you. For everything.

options.text: You're welcome, Cassia.

introduction: *She returns to her work, more at peace than you've ever seen her*
The Violet Mortar is open for business.
What do you need?

options.text: I'd like to see your inventory.
Just wanted to see how you were doing.
Nothing right now. Take care.

introduction: *She smiles genuinely*
I'm... managing. Better than I was.
Some days are harder than others, but the work helps.
Thank you for asking.

options.text: Good to hear. Stay well.

introduction: *Cassia looks toward the horizon, a sad smile on her face*
He's gone. Fled after the battle, I'm told.
Part of me hoped he would stay. That we could find a way forward.
*She turns to you*
But perhaps distance is what he needs. Time to heal, to remember who he was.
Thank you for giving him that chance.

options.text: [Offer condolences]

introduction: *Cassia's eyes are red from crying*
He's dead. The Shadow Blight finally took him completely.
*She wipes her eyes*
I held a private memorial. For the man he once was, not the monster he became.
Thank you for... ending his torment. It was a kindness, in the end.

options.text: [Offer condolences]

introduction: *Cassia's face lights up with bittersweet hope*
You saw it, didn't you? In the final moment, he fought WITH you.
The Marcus I loved was still there. Buried, but not gone.
*Tears stream down her face*
He gave his life to weaken the portal. A final act of redemption.
That's how I'll remember him. Not as a cultist, but as a hero.

options.text: [Console her]

introduction: *She sets down her mortar*
I'm managing. Same as always.
*A pause*
It helps, having customers who care. Makes the days easier.

options.text: I'm glad.
